# Inverse Adding-Doubling 3-11-4 (15 Oct 2019) 
# 
#                        Beam diameter =     7.0 mm
#                     Sample thickness =   1.500 mm
#                  Top slide thickness =   1.250 mm
#               Bottom slide thickness =   1.250 mm
#           Sample index of refraction =  1.3300
#        Top slide index of refraction =  1.4600
#     Bottom slide index of refraction =  1.4600
# 
#    Fraction unscattered refl. in M_R =   100.0 %
#   Fraction unscattered trans. in M_T =   100.0 %
# 
# Reflection sphere
#                      sphere diameter =    76.2 mm
#                 sample port diameter =    15.0 mm
#               entrance port diameter =    13.0 mm
#               detector port diameter =     3.2 mm
#                     wall reflectance =   100.0 %
#                 standard reflectance =   100.0 %
#                 detector reflectance =     0.0 %
#
# Transmission sphere
#                      sphere diameter =    76.2 mm
#                 sample port diameter =    15.0 mm
#               entrance port diameter =    13.0 mm
#               detector port diameter =     3.2 mm
#                     wall reflectance =   100.0 %
#               standard transmittance =   100.0 %
#                 detector reflectance =     0.0 %
#
# M_R and M_T were measured using the comparison (dual-beam) method.
# Single sphere corrections were used with light incident at 8 degrees from the normal.
# The inverse routine adapted to the input data.
# 
# 
#                 AD quadrature points =  12
#             AD tolerance for success =   0.00010
